description: baseline -- no automated security tooling in project; govulncheck not installed
/admin/claude/callback unauthenticated by design -- echoes params only, no state change
Admin token accepted via query param (log/referer leak) + debug mode logs configured token
Friend code grants access to account management (add/refresh/exchange) across all providers
PKCE verifier passed as OAuth state parameter (visible in redirect URL)
No per-user rate limiting on proxy requests; one user can exhaust all accounts
Raw client IP stored alongside hashed origin ID in BoltDB
Debug mode logs both provided and configured admin tokens in plaintext
Refresh token random part never validated; any valid user ID -> full credentials
State-changing POST endpoints lack CSRF protection (mitigated by JSON Content-Type)
Claude pool tokens never expire; Codex tokens expire in 10 years
JWT secret "bigfarts" in sample config (local file only, not in git)
Refresh proxy credentials in plaintext URL (local config only)
Proxy headers trusted without validation when not behind Cloudflare
Pool user token in URL path for config download (browser history/log exposure)
JSON body decoded without size limits on admin/friend endpoints
No structured audit logging; admin actions logged to stdout only
No user-controllable URLs in server-side requests
Dependencies appear current; govulncheck unavailable for automated scan
Fake OAuth generates alg:none JWT; no security impact (always replaced before upstream)
Race condition in friend claim: concurrent requests may create duplicate users
Friend code -> user ID enumeration -> refresh forgery -> full account takeover
Pool user IDs exposed via /api/pool/users (friend code auth)
WebSocket InsecureSkipVerify:true (intentional for CLI clients)
Pool stats expose account IDs, plan types, usage with friend code auth
No CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, HSTS, Referrer-Policy
User IDs are random hex but exposed via API (predictability is moot)
Passthrough mode allows proxy use as anonymizing relay with own credentials
readBodyForReplay reads up to 16MB per request into memory
Gemini API key HMAC truncated to 96 bits (still adequate)
All friend-auth endpoints open when no admin_token and no friend_code configured
Claude trace can capture auth headers to disk when TRACE_INCLUDE_SECRETS=1
